info.title: Hovel Daemon RPC
info.summary: Stable HTTP/JSON contract between hoveld and Hovel front ends.
info.description: The Hovel daemon RPC is the stable local service contract used by the CLI, MCP adapter, one-shot execution, and future front ends. It is transported as HTTP POST with JSON request and response bodies over the daemon listener.

x-hovel-json-field-casing: Use the field names shown here exactly. Older daemon structs without json tags intentionally expose PascalCase JSON fields; newer lower-camel fields are explicitly tagged in source.

servers.description: Logical base URL used by local Unix-domain-socket clients.
Example loopback TCP listener for trusted integration or remote-adapter development; it is not a public authenticated endpoint.

tags.name: execution
tags.description: Module execution and payload-provider bridge calls.

tags.name: sessions
tags.description: Interactive module session lifecycle and I/O.

tags.name: operator-session
tags.description: Operation, chain, target, config, step, snapshot, and log state.

tags.name: entities
tags.description: Live operator entity attachment and launch-key approval state.

tags.name: mesh
tags.description: Provider-owned Mesh listener lifecycle, topology, task, stream, beacon, and daemon bookkeeping calls.

tags.name: pki
tags.description: Workspace certificate authority, issuance, signing lease, inventory, and explicit bundle export operations.

MeshBridgeOpenRequest.description: Open a daemon-owned local TCP or UDP loopback endpoint backed by one Mesh session flow.

MeshBridgeCloseRequest.description: Close a daemon-owned Mesh bridge by supplying exactly one operation or session id.

TextError.description: Plain-text daemon error returned by http.Error.

SessionChunk.description: Session bytes, encoded as base64 by Go JSON.

SessionCommandListResponse.description: Provider-owned commands for an active session.

PollLogsResponse.description: Bounded log stream cursor and entries.

MeshStreamOpenResponse.description: Session opened through a Mesh stream.

MeshBridgeOpenResponse.description: Local endpoint for a daemon-owned Mesh bridge session flow.

Cache-Control.description: The response contains an ephemeral bearer capability and must not be cached.

MeshListenerStartResponse.description: Started or attached Mesh listening post and audit operation.

MeshListenerStopResponse.description: Stopped or detached Mesh listening post and audit operation.

PKICertificateLifecycleResult.description: Retry-safe certificate renewal or key rotation result.

PKICertificateRevocationResult.description: Retry-safe certificate revocation result with all assignment effects.

PKIRevocationListResponse.description: Revocations issued by one authority in recording order.

PKICRLPublicationResult.description: Retry-safe completed CRL publication and immutable signed generation.

PKICRLListResponse.description: CRL generations for one authority, ordered by CRL number and ID.

PKICRLPublicationIntent.description: Durable CRL publication plan after reconciliation.

PKICRLPublicationListResponse.description: Stale CRL publication plans reconciled in this batch.

PKIOperationListResponse.description: Canonical PKI operations in deterministic order.

PKIOperationInspection.description: One PKI operation with its durable consumer acknowledgements and current acknowledgement gaps.

PKICredentialStampListResponse.description: Secret-free credential stamp plans and terminal evidence in deterministic order.

PKICredentialStamp.description: One secret-free credential stamp plan and its current lifecycle state.

PKICredentialExecutionListResponse.description: Secret-free runtime, file-delivery, and provider-encoding execution records in deterministic order.

PKICredentialExecution.description: One secret-free credential provider execution record.

PKICanonicalUint64.description: Canonical base-10 uint64 string; leading zeroes are forbidden.

ByteString.description: Base64-encoded bytes produced by Go JSON for []byte fields.

operation.description: Omit to keep the previous operation; send an empty string to clear it.

activeChain.description: Omit to keep the previous chain; send an empty string to clear it.

heartbeatTimeout.description: Go duration string, for example 30s or 2m.

AgentHint.description: Untrusted module-authored guidance; it never bypasses planning, guardrails, or confirmation.

providerStatus.description: Provider-defined task outcome retained separately from the closed daemon lifecycle state.
Provider-defined task outcome retained separately from the closed daemon lifecycle state.

listenerState.description: Provider-defined listener state; standard values are starting, active, stopping, stopped, and failed.

kind.description: Common values are survey, upload, execute, upload_execute, command, load, and stream; providers may define additional kinds.
Provider-advertised task kind; common values are survey, upload, execute, upload_execute, command, load, and stream.

status.description: Provider-defined terminal status; standard values are succeeded and failed.

localHost.description: Loopback IP address; empty or localhost selects 127.0.0.1.

capability.description: Ephemeral 256-bit bearer capability. Send capability plus LF as the first TCP bytes or as the first complete UDP datagram; Hovel consumes the handshake and never forwards it to the provider. Do not log or persist this value.

MeshListener.description: Provider-reported listening post. Start configuration and credentials are intentionally absent.

state.description: Provider-defined state; standard values are starting, active, stopping, stopped, and failed.

config.description: Provider-specific write-only configuration; never returned by listener read models.

MeshCredentialSelections.description: Non-secret assignment and slot selections resolved by the daemon immediately before the Mesh operation. The array never carries credential bytes, references, paths, provider targets, or receipts.

requestId.description: Stable idempotency identity for this credential delivery. Reuse it only when retrying the same selection and Mesh operation.

capability.description: The first production selector path supports same-process runtime delivery only. File materialization and stamping use their dedicated contracts.

approveCredentialUse.description: Explicitly authorizes assignment-bound credential delivery into the selected provider process; no secret material is returned to the caller.

approveCrlPublicationReconciliation.description: Explicitly authorizes atomic failure reconciliation of stale CRL publication plans.

PKIOID.description: Canonical dotted ASN.1 object identifier. Domain validation also enforces the first- and second-arc bounds.

PKISignatureAlgorithm.description: Certificate signature algorithm. An empty value or auto delegates selection to the selected crypto backend.

PKIConcreteSignatureAlgorithm.description: Concrete signature algorithm durably committed before CRL signing.

PKIDistinguishedName.description: Certificate subject. At least one name field or extraNames entry must be non-empty.

PKISubjectAlternativeNames.description: Typed DNS, IP, email, and URI subject alternative names; the aggregate list is limited to 256 entries.

PKINameConstraints.description: Typed CA name constraints; all lists together are limited to 256 entries.

existingKeyId.description: Required only when source is existing; rejected for every other source.

maxPathLenZero.description: Distinguishes an explicit zero path-length constraint from an omitted constraint.

value.description: One to 64 decoded bytes when mode is explicit; rejected for automatic and omitted modes.

der.description: Non-empty DER value. Typed certificate extension OIDs are rejected here.

serialNumber.description: Canonical positive hexadecimal X.509 serial number, limited to 20 DER content bytes.

notBefore.description: UTC certificate validity start with whole-second precision.

notAfter.description: UTC certificate validity end with whole-second precision and later than notBefore.

keyUsage.description: X.509 key-usage bit mask: digital-signature=1, content-commitment=2, key-encipherment=4, data-encipherment=8, key-agreement=16, certificate-sign=32, crl-sign=64, encipher-only=128, decipher-only=256.

template.description: Optional complete typed certificate template. When omitted, Hovel preserves names and usages, allocates a fresh serial and validity window, and binds the existing key.
Optional complete typed certificate template. When omitted, Hovel preserves names and usages, allocates a fresh serial and validity window, and requests a new key.

keyReused.description: True only for renewal, which reuses the source key.

effectiveAt.description: Optional effective time. When omitted, the daemon records its current time. Future times are rejected.

affectedAssignments.description: Complete sorted assignment updates committed atomically with the revocation.

validitySeconds.description: Zero or omission selects the 24-hour default; non-zero values are bounded to 5 minutes through 7 days.

signatureAlgorithm.description: Omission or auto resolves deterministically from the issuer key before the plan is persisted.

activeTrustGenerationId.description: Immutable trust generation pinned to the active certificate generation.

stagedTrustGenerationId.description: Immutable trust generation pinned to the staged certificate generation.

rotationPolicyId.description: Reserved for lifecycle policies; non-empty values are rejected until policy inventory is implemented.

crlGenerationIds.description: Immutable CRL generations. Every CRL must be fresh and its issuer must be present in this trust generation.
